meanings.definition: a substance used to ignite or kindle a fire
a device for lighting or igniting fuel or charges or fires
a flatbottom boat for carrying heavy loads (especially on canals)
transport in a flatbottom boat
of comparatively little physical weight or density
examples: magnesium is a light metal--having a specific gravity of 1.74 at 20 degrees C

meanings.definition: (used of color) having a relatively small amount of coloring agent
of the military or industry; using (or being) relatively small or light arms or equipment
not great in degree or quantity or number
psychologically light; especially free from sadness or troubles
characterized by or emitting light
examples: a room that is light when the shutters are open
the inside of the house was airy and light

meanings.definition: (used of vowels or syllables) pronounced with little or no stress
examples: a syllable that ends in a short vowel is a light syllable
a weak stress on the second syllable

meanings.definition: easily assimilated in the alimentary canal; not rich or heavily seasoned
(used of soil) loose and large-grained in consistency
(of sound or color) free from anything that dulls or dims
examples: efforts to obtain a clean bass in orchestral recordings
a light lilting voice like a silver bell

meanings.definition: moving easily and quickly; nimble
examples: the dancer was light and graceful
walked with a light tripping step

meanings.definition: demanding little effort; not burdensome
of little intensity or power or force
examples: the light touch of her fingers

meanings.definition: (physics, chemistry) not having atomic weight greater than average
weak and likely to lose consciousness
examples: suddenly felt faint from the pain
was sick and faint from hunger

meanings.definition: very thin and insubstantial
marked by temperance in indulgence
examples: abstemious with the use of adverbs

meanings.definition: less than the correct or legal or full amount often deliberately so
having little importance
examples: losing his job was no light matter

meanings.definition: intended primarily as entertainment; not serious or profound
silly or trivial
designed for ease of movement or to carry little weight
having relatively few calories
(of sleep) easily disturbed
casual and unrestrained in sexual behavior
examples: he was told to avoid loose (or light) women
